actors.name: Saami Council
actors.description: Voluntary NGO promoting Sami rights and interests across Norway, Sweden, Finland, and Russia. Permanent participant in the Arctic Council representing over 100,000 Sami people.
actors.contact_pathway: Intersects with Naturskyddsforeningen's Swedish environmental policy goals
actors.notes: Trans-national biosphere-aligned governance structure operating outside Westphalian nation-state boundaries. Direct collaborator candidate.

intel.value: Green colonialism advocacy; co-authored report with Amnesty International on human rights violations tied to green energy exploitation in Sapmi; environmental advocacy on climate change adaptation and biodiversity
Arctic Council (Permanent Participant), UNFCCC, Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD), Amnesty International
Operates across Norway, Sweden, Finland covering Sapmi region; secretariat in Karasjok, Norway
Foundational for prototyping indigenous governance, rights of nature, and non-state planetary representation. Living prototype for General Assembly of Earth.
Trans-national indigenous governance body representing Sami people across four countries; integrates traditional ecological knowledge with international climate policy
Co-authored report with Amnesty International on green colonialism and human rights violations in Sapmi

actors.name: Rights of Nature Sweden
actors.description: Non-profit advocating for recognition of nature's inherent rights within Swedish law, part of global movement for Earth jurisprudence. Also known as Naturens Rattigheter.
actors.contact_pathway: Same Swedish ecosystem as Naturskyddsforeningen and Global Challenges Foundation
actors.notes: Actively attempting to rewrite Swedish legal foundations to include non-human actors. Direct collaborator candidate.

intel.value: Advocating for rights of Lake Vattern; drafted proposed amendment to Swedish Constitution to guarantee nature the right to exist, thrive, regenerate, and evolve (2019)
End Ecocide Sweden, Lodyn, Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature (GARN), Community Environmental Legal Defense Fund (CELDF), UN Harmony with Nature Knowledge Network
Swedish-based organization working on Swedish constitutional reform; coordinator Pella Thiel is central node in European rights of nature movement
Provides legal templates and philosophical grounding for planetary governance including biosphere as stakeholder
Drafted proposed constitutional amendment with Rebecka Le Moine (Swedish Green Party MP) and international legal experts

people.skills: Arctic environmental policy, climate change, traditional ecological knowledge
people.job_title: Head of Arctic and Environmental Unit

people.skills: Rights of nature advocacy, legal reform, ecocide legislation, movement building
people.notes: Central node in European rights of nature movement. Named WWF Sweden Environmental Hero of the Year 2019. Represents Swedish movement within UN Harmony with Nature Knowledge Network.

people.skills: Rights of nature advocacy, legal and philosophical frameworks

intel.value: Operates in same Swedish ecosystem as Rights of Nature Sweden and Saami Council; intersects with Saami Council's environmental defense strategies

existing_actor_intel.actor_name: Global Alliance for the Rights of Nature

intel.value: Rights of Nature Sweden (Naturens Rattigheter) is a partner organization in Sweden

search_phrases.phrase: Rights of Nature Sweden constitutional amendment Lake Vattern
Pella Thiel rights of nature UN Harmony
